greens_speed_ramp_plan.objective: Increase green speed for an event without collapsing plant health or surface consistency.
greens_speed_ramp_plan.prep_window: Start 7-10 days before the event and make changes in small, observable increments.
prep_steps: Confirm baseline moisture, clipping yield, and ball roll before making changes.
Reduce one stressor at a time: mowing height, frequency, rolling, or nitrogen.
Use light topdressing and venting early in the prep window if smoothness is needed.
Tighten irrigation precision so firmness gains do not become wilt episodes.
during_event: Use hand watering and dew management aggressively to protect consistency.
Do not stack double cuts, extra rolling, and aggressive dry-down on the same day without data.
Watch weak collars, cleanup laps, and shaded greens first.
recovery_steps: Restore moisture uniformly before adding recovery fertility.
Back off rolling and mowing intensity in stages.
Use light nitrogen only after hydration and canopy stress are stabilized.
failure_modes: wilting disguised as desired firmness
localized dry spot expansion
anthracnose or scalping on weak poa areas
surface inconsistency from chasing a single speed number

firmness_and_moisture_tournament_plan.objective: Deliver predictable firmness and bounce without slipping into chronic wilt or soft, puffy surfaces.
firmness_and_moisture_tournament_plan.prep_window: Begin 5-7 days ahead using daily moisture checks and event-specific target bands.
prep_steps: Set moisture ranges by green rather than forcing one blanket target.
Map weak spots, LDS zones, and shaded pockets before event week.
Coordinate wetting-agent, hand-watering, and rolling plans instead of treating them separately.
Use venting only when it improves gas exchange more than it harms presentation.
during_event: Track firmness and volumetric water content at the same time each day.
Correct hot spots surgically rather than syringing entire surfaces.
Communicate target adjustments after weather shifts instead of pretending the plan is static.
recovery_steps: Return greens to normal moisture bands gradually.
Document greens that needed repeated rescue watering for future prep refinement.
Repair soft or hydrophobic areas before the next stress cycle.
failure_modes: uniformity errors masked as moisture strategy
afternoon wilt on exposed ridges
soft pockets from overcorrecting morning firmness
hand-watering inconsistency between crews

fairway_tournament_presentation_plan.objective: Improve visual presentation and play quality while keeping fairways healthy under traffic.
fairway_tournament_presentation_plan.prep_window: Start 7-14 days before the event depending on fertility, mowing, and growth rate.
prep_steps: Dial mowing frequency and clipping management before tightening height.
Clean up edges, patterns, and weak turns that will be magnified during event week.
Use growth regulation carefully so striping and density do not outrun recovery capacity.
Schedule irrigation around firmness goals and cart traffic paths.
during_event: Protect landing zones and turn areas from repeated compaction.
Monitor wilt on high spots, especially after presentation mowing.
Keep divot recovery and hand-detail work consistent across the course.
recovery_steps: Relieve traffic concentration quickly if soils sealed over.
Restore moderate fertility and irrigation uniformity to stressed corridors.
Assess whether presentation goals exposed weak irrigation or mowing logistics.
failure_modes: scalping on crowned fairways
cart turn wear and localized compaction
softness after cosmetic irrigation
mismatched growth regulation causing uneven presentation

event_recovery_greens_plan.objective: Bring greens back from tournament conditioning without creating rebound softness or disease.
event_recovery_greens_plan.prep_window: Begin recovery immediately after the event and manage the first 72 hours intentionally.
prep_steps: Take final event measurements so recovery can be compared to actual stress load.
Identify greens with chronic wilt, cleanup abrasion, or traffic injury before broad changes.
Stage recovery around hydration, mowing normalization, and rootzone oxygen.
during_event: Record repeated rescue watering, wilt sites, and mower stress notes for each green.
recovery_steps: Reduce rolling first, then normalize mowing frequency and height.
Use light nutrition and venting if roots and gas exchange were stressed.
Watch for disease on canopies that were pushed hard and then rewatered.
failure_modes: overwatering after event week
flush growth from excessive recovery nitrogen
disease following stress plus rehydration
ignoring weak collars and cleanup arcs

event_recovery_fairway_plan.objective: Recover density and playability after concentrated event traffic and presentation stress.
event_recovery_fairway_plan.prep_window: Start immediately post-event, with the biggest corrections in the first week.
prep_steps: Map traffic lanes, cart turns, and thin presentation areas while evidence is fresh.
Assess whether irrigation, compaction, or mowing contributed most to visible decline.
during_event: Track high-traffic bottlenecks and any wilt-prone landing areas.
recovery_steps: Use cultivation or venting where soils sealed under traffic.
Return to agronomically normal irrigation instead of cosmetic event watering.
Feed only enough to restore density without creating soft, disease-prone flushes.
Repair wear areas before they become weed-entry points.
failure_modes: lingering compaction in turns and exits
uneven recovery from event-only irrigation patterns
weed invasion into thin areas
overgrowth after heavy recovery fertility

weather_disruption_event_plan.objective: Adjust tournament prep and presentation calmly when heat, storms, or humidity break the original plan.
weather_disruption_event_plan.prep_window: Use as a contingency plan any time weather shifts during the final 72 hours.
prep_steps: Identify which goals are negotiable: speed, firmness, visual uniformity, or labor intensity.
Pre-stage hand-watering, moisture testing, and communication roles before disruption arrives.
Protect surfaces first, then rebuild presentation where conditions allow.
during_event: After storms, evaluate surface consistency and mower safety before resuming standard setup.
During heat spikes, trade a little speed for plant survival early rather than emergency rescue late.
Communicate plan changes across the crew so moisture and mowing decisions stay aligned.
recovery_steps: Document what weather forced you to abandon and what actually mattered most to conditions.
Repair weak areas created by emergency traffic or water movement as soon as possible.
failure_modes: sticking to the original plan after conditions invalidated it
mixed crew decisions on water and mowing
surface inconsistency after storm cleanup
heat injury from refusing to give back a little speed
